relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The wilderness journey is gathered into remembered stages.
Moses writes departures as carefully as destinations.
Israel leaves Rameses in the wake of Passover with uplifted hand.
Egypt buries its firstborn while its gods are judged.
Rameses gives way to Succoth.
Succoth gives way to Etham at the wilderness edge.
The route bends back toward Pi-hahiroth and Migdol.
The people cross the sea and enter three days of wilderness before Marah.
Elim offers twelve springs and seventy palms.
The Sea of Reeds becomes another camp.
The wilderness of Sin enters the map.
Dophkah is remembered.
Alush is remembered.
Rephidim is remembered as a place without water.
Sinai becomes a camp of revelation.
The Graves of Craving become a station rather than the end of the people.
Hazeroth follows.
Rithmah follows.
Rimmon-perez follows.
Libnah follows.
Rissah follows.
Kehelathah follows.
Mount Shapher follows.
Haradah follows.
Makheloth follows.
Tahath follows.
Terah follows.
Mithkah follows.
Hashmonah follows.
Moseroth follows.
Bene-jaakan follows.
Hor-haggidgad follows.
Jotbathah follows.
Abronah follows.
Ezion-geber follows.
Kadesh gathers the long wilderness into one charged place.
Mount Hor stands at Edom's edge.
Aaron climbs, dies, and is dated within the journey.
His one hundred twenty-three years are remembered.
Arad hears that Israel is approaching.
Zalmonah follows Aaron's death.
Punon follows.
Oboth follows.
Iye-abarim marks Moab's border.
Dibon-gad follows.
Almon-diblathaim follows.
The mountains of Abarim face Nebo.
The plains of Moab become the final wilderness encampment.
The people stretch along Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim.
At the threshold of crossing, YHWH speaks again.
The command turns from remembered travel toward contested land.
The text orders inhabitants displaced and their sacred sites destroyed.
Possession of the land is framed as Divine gift.
Population and lot shape the internal distribution.
Those who remain are described as wounds and threats.
The conquest warning turns back upon Israel if the command is incomplete.

contemplative_note: Numbers 33 is most trustworthy as a map of memory: every camp says the people survived another departure. Its final conquest commands must not be spiritualized into permission to uproot real communities or destroy their sacred places.
